LAW OFFICES

OLIVER, STOEVER, BARR & VOSE
THOMAS W. STOEVER
WILLIAM E. BARR
CHARLES S. VOSE
CONNIE COOKE SANDIFER
ROBERT W. OLIVER
EDWARD W. LEE
HERIBERTO F. DIAZ
JAMES DUFF MURPHY
JANICE R. MIYAHIRA

A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

1900 SUNSET BOULEVARD

LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012

(213) 250-3043

TELECOPIER
(213) 482-5336

July 13, 1990

Susan L. Bobrow, Esq.
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for Information Assistance for
the City of Hermosa Beach

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

I am transmitting this letter in response to your July 3,
1990 correspondence providing this office with certain informal
assistance (Your File No. I-90-415). In reading your advice
letter, I am left with the apparent obligation to inform all
councilmembers that even if they have a month-to-month tenancy,
they should excuse themselves on the basis of a potential
conflict of interest.

In your July 3, 1990 letter, it is stated that the
difference between fair market value and a "discounted rent"
would constitute a gift, and if that amount is more than
$250.00 in the last twelve (12) months, any recipient of that
gift would have a conflict of interest. From a practical point
of view, it is often difficult, if not impossible, to determine
"market rate" for similar rentals in an area surrounding a
subject rental unit. If you assume a market rental of
$1,000.00 per month, a 3% variation in market rate ($30.00 per
month) would constitute a sufficient conflict of interest for
a councilmember who resides on property by a month-to-month
tenancy.

It seems to me that even if a councilmember has a month-
to-month tenancy, such councilmember must conclude that he or
she has a conflict of interest unless they can prove their
rental is within $25.00 of the then current market rate without
risk of being challenged.

In advising various city council that we represent, this
firm has always given conflict of interest advice on a very
conservative basis. As a result of the information which you
have provided, we will have no alternative but to advise all
councilmembers that if they have any interest in real property,

OLIVER, STOEVER, BARR & VOSE

Susan L. Bobrow, Esq.
July 13, 1990
Page 2.


including a month-to-month rental, they should declare a
conflict of interest unless they can prove that their current
rental is within $25.00 of the existing market rate for the
area. If in discussing this matter with the other attorneys
in the legal division, it is the opinion that this advice is
too conservative, I would appreciate receiving any comments
that you may have.

Thank you for your prior response and cooperation
regarding this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Charles S. Vose
of OLIVER, STOEVER, BARR & VOSE

CSV:ilf

cc: Scott Hallabrin, Esq., Acting General Counsel

LAW OFFICES

OLIVER, STOEVER, BARR & YOSE
THOMAS W. STOEVER
WILLIAM M. BARR
CHARLES S. VOSE
CONNIE COOKE SANDIFER
ROGER W. SPRINGER
EDWARD W. LEE
HERIBERTO F. DIAZ
JAMES DUFF MURPHY
JANICE R. MIYAMURA

A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
1000 SUNSET BOULEVARD
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012
(213) 250-3043

TELECOPIER
(213) 482-5336

June 11, 1990

John Rothman, Esq.
Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800, Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

RE: REQUEST FOR CONFLICT OF INTEREST OPINION -
CITY OF HERMOSA BEACH

Dear Mr. Rothman:

I have been requested by the impacted City Councilmember to
obtain an opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission
concerning an alleged conflict of interest issue. This relates to
a matter currently pending before the Hermosa Beach City Council
in which it is proposed that certain property be rezoned.

A councilmember owns property within 300 feet of the affected
area. This city councilmember has abstained from voting or
participating on the basis of a potential conflict of interest.
A second councilmember (Kathleen Midstokke) resides, as a tenant,
at the property which is owned by the abstaining councilmember.
(She has resided at this location for years prior to her election
to the City Council.)

It is alleged that Councilmember Midstokke has a conflict of
interest in that she rents property within 300 feet of the property
subject to the zone change. It is also alleged that Councilmember
Midstokke has a potential conflict of interest due to the fact that
she is a tenant in a residence owned by another Councilmember who
has declared a conflict on the matter. There is no evidence to
suggest that the rezoning (or a denial of the rezoning) would have
a material financial impact on Councilmember Midstokke's rental of
her current residence.

OLIVER, STOEVER, BARR & VOSE


This matter is scheduled to be heard by the city council at
their first meeting in July. We would appreciate receiving your
opinion prior to June 30, 1990. Should you have further questions
or require additional information please do not hesitate to contact
me.

Thank you for your anticipated consideration of this request.

Very truly yours,

OLIVER, STOEVER, BARR & VOSE

Charlie S. Vose

CHARLES S. VOSE

CC: Kathleen Midstokke, City Councilmember
